title: Deguelia floribunda
taxonomy: Deguelia floribunda is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as legumes. It is a member of the subfamily Faboideae and is native to tropical Africa. Further classification places Deguelia floribunda in the tribe Dalbergieae. The plant is also known by its synonym Dalbergia floribunda. The exact classification may vary depending on the source, but it is generally categorized as a type of leguminous tree or shrub.
uses: Deguelia floribunda has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes. Its leaves and bark are used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The plant's extracts have been reported to have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which could aid in treating infections. Additionally, Deguelia floribunda is believed to have antioxidant properties, which may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. More research is needed to fully understand the medicinal potential of this plant.
benefits: Deguelia floribunda has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in South America, for its potential health benefits. The plant is said to possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. It may help alleviate symptoms of various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Deguelia floribunda for medicinal purposes. Further studies are necessary to fully understand its potential health benefits and potential side effects.

constituents: Deguelia floribunda contains a range of active constituents, including alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids. These compounds are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties and potential health benefits. Alkaloids like deguelin and 4-hydroxydeguelin have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. Flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, may contribute to the plant's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Further research is needed to fully understand the composition and activity of Deguelia floribunda's constituents.

intro: Deguelia floribunda, also known as the "Guayacanillo" or "Cedro de la montaña", is a plant species native to Central and South America, belonging to the Fabaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Deguelia floribunda include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are often used to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
